metadata.title: Ubuntu LTS Security Audit — Assessment Plan
metadata.remarks: Assessment Plan for ubuntu-sec-audit automated checks. Referenced by generated OSCAL Assessment Results documents.

import-ssp.remarks: Replace with a relative path to your System Security Plan, e.g. ./ssp.json

activities.title: Automated Security Checks
activities.description: Automated bash-based checks covering package management, firewall, SSH, users, kernel parameters, file permissions, PAM, auditing, and service configuration.

steps.title: Execute ubuntu-sec-audit.sh
steps.description: Run the ubuntu-sec-audit.sh script with --oscal flag as root. The script performs all standard (and optionally deep) checks, collecting structured findings in a JSONL file.

steps.title: Generate OSCAL Assessment Results
steps.description: The script automatically invokes oscal/oscal_generate.py to produce an OSCAL 1.1.2 AR document from the collected JSONL findings.
steps.remarks: Output: oscal-ar-YYYYMMDD-HHMM.json in the report directory.

steps.title: Review and Remediate
steps.description: Review the generated Markdown report and OSCAL AR. Apply fixes from the auto-generated fix script. Re-run to verify remediation.

reviewed-controls.remarks: All controls mapped in mappings/control-mapping.json are reviewed. Use --profile to select a subset.

assessment-subjects.description: Ubuntu LTS system under assessment (20.04, 22.04, or 24.04)

tasks.title: Run ubuntu-sec-audit (Standard Mode)
tasks.description: Execute standard mode audit with OSCAL output

tasks.title: Run ubuntu-sec-audit (Deep Mode)
tasks.description: Execute deep mode audit (adds rkhunter, Lynis, ClamAV, debsums, full SUID scan)
